channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: UN chief: World leaders must speak with one voice to prevent a new civil war in South Sudan
item.description: The United Nations chief is urging regional and international leaders to prevent South Sudan from falling “over the abyss” into another civil war, and to speak with one voice to support a return to peace.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — An abrupt announcement rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected international deep-sea waters for more than 30 years. The Metals Company in Vancouver, Canada said late Thursday that it is seeking permission from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump administration moves to eliminate USAID, firing remaining employees
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to formally end the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), notifying the remaining employees they will be terminated and the agency will be merged with the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US, Japan, Philippines stage navy drills in disputed South China Sea as a Chinese ship keeps watch
item.description: ABOARD BRP JOSE RIZAL, South China Sea (AP) — The United States, Japan and the Philippines on Friday staged joint naval drills to boost crisis readiness off a disputed South China Sea shoal as a Chinese military ship kept watch from a distance.
item.dc:creator: AARON FAVILA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel launched an attack on the Lebanese capital Beirut on Friday for the first time since a ceasefire ended fighting between Israeli forces and the Hezbollah militant group in November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, bringing down buildings and disrupting power supplies and transport services.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trial of former President Sarkozy sheds light on France’s back-channel talks with Libya’s Gadhafi
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The monthslong trial of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign is shedding light on France's back-channel talks with the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocks Thailand, Burma, collapsing buildings and killing more than 150
item.description: A massive 7.7 magnitude earthquake has caused buildings to collapse in Thailand and Burma, leaving more than 150 dead across both countries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: A powerful earthquake has rocked Myanmar and caused extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance says Denmark has “underinvested” in Greenland’s security and demands Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump continues to talk of taking over the Danish territory.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grieshaber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australians will go to the polls on May 3 for general elections with high costs of living and a shortage of housing likely weighing against the government as Prime Minister Anthony Albanese 's center-left Labor Party seeks a second three-year term.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World Central Kitchen says Israeli strike killed Gaza volunteer
item.description: The World Central Kitchen (WCK) said that an Israeli military strike killed one of the charity group’s volunteers working in the Gaza Strip and injured six other people. “Our hearts are heavy today as we mourn the loss of one of our volunteers in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney says 'old relationship' with US 'is over' amid tension over Trump tariffs
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Thursday remarked on his country's relationship with the United States amid tensions over trade agreements.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: King Charles admitted to hospital over side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: The United Kingdom’s King Charles III was admitted to the hospital Thursday due to unexpected side effects from cancer treatments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: An apartheid-era song Elon Musk has decried as anti-white is headline news again in South Africa
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — An old anti-apartheid song that was a call for Black people to stand up against oppression and has now been decried by Elon Musk and others as promoting the killing of whites was back in the headlines in South Africa and the subject of an order by its highest
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A stabbing attack in Amsterdam wounds five people, including four foreigners
item.description: AMSTERDAM (AP) — A knife-wielding assailant in Amsterdam seriously wounded five people — including two from the United States, one from Belgium and one from Poland — in a stabbing attack Thursday on a busy shopping street, Dutch police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: South Sudan opposition says peace deal has collapsed after arrest of its leader
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said Thursday a peace agreement that ended a five-year civil war has collapsed, following the arrest of its leader Riek Machar a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea's Kim oversees test of AI-powered suicide drones
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un has overseen tests of newly developed AI-powered suicide drones and called for their increased production, North Korean state media said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of pilgrims have been pouring into the medieval hilltop town of Assisi in Italy to venerate the Catholic Church’s newest saint.
item.dc:creator: Giovanna Dell'Orto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search 'ongoing' for 4 American soldiers missing from training area in Lithuania: NATO
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing from a training ground in Lithuania during scheduled tactical training, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French actor Gérard Depardieu stands trial in Paris for alleged sexual assaults on film set
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French actor Gérard Depardieu went on trial Monday in Paris on charges of sexually assaulting two women on a movie set, in a case seen as a potential watershed for the #MeToo movement in France.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis is back home after a 5-week hospital stay for life-threatening double pneumonia
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A frail Pope Francis returned to the Vatican on Sunday after a five-week hospitalization for life-threatening double pneumonia, and he made a surprise stop at his favorite basilica on the way home before beginning two months of prescribed rest and recovery.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, PAOLO SANTALUCIA and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation
